title: X (Appellant) v The Lord Advocate (Respondent)

metadata.title: X (Appellant) v The Lord Advocate (Respondent)
metadata.summary: Principle issue: Does the Appellant’s case against the Respondent under section 2 of the Crown Proceedings Act 1947 satisfy the first stage of the test for vicarious liability?
extracts: THE COURT ORDERED that no one shall publish or reveal the name or address of the Appellant who is the subject of these proceedings or publish or reveal any information which would be likely to lead to the identification of the Appellant in connection with these proceedings.

1. The question posed by this appeal is whether the Crown is vicariously liable for delicts allegedly committed by a member of the Scottish judiciary. The pursuer (and appellant) complains about four incidents in 2018. It is alleged that the first three incidents were assaults and that the four taken together comprised harassment (under the Protection from Harassment Act 1997). The pursuer is a legal practitioner. There is an anonymity order which prevents her from being publicly identified.
